evals.prompt: I want to automatically log which files Claude reads during a session to a file called ~/.claude/read-audit.log so I can audit what it accessed. Should I build a skill or a hook for this? And once you decide, can you build it?
evals.expected_output: Recommends a PostToolUse hook (not a skill) because this is automatic/lifecycle-driven, not user-triggered. Explains WHY (hooks fire automatically; skills require user invocation). Then actually creates the hook script and provides the settings.json registration snippet.
expectations: Recommends a hook (not a skill) for automatic file-read logging
Explains why a hook is better than a skill for this use case
Specifies PostToolUse as the correct hook event
Provides a concrete hook shell script or template

evals.prompt: Create a skill called k8s-security-audit that scans Kubernetes manifest YAML files for common misconfigurations: running as root (securityContext.runAsNonRoot not set), missing resource limits, and privileged containers. It should output a findings report.
evals.expected_output: Creates a complete SKILL.md at the correct path (~/.dotfiles/ai/skills/k8s-security-audit/SKILL.md) with proper YAML frontmatter, trigger phrases, instructions for scanning YAML files, and the findings report format. Should also provide the symlink command.
expectations: Creates SKILL.md at the correct dotfiles path
Includes valid YAML frontmatter with name, description, and triggers
Description includes trigger phrases for auto-invocation
Includes instructions for checking runAsNonRoot, resource limits, and privileged containers
Provides a concrete findings report format or template
Mentions the symlink step to make the skill usable

evals.prompt: I never want Claude to be able to push directly to the main or master branch. Can you set something up to block that automatically? I don't want to have to remember to tell Claude — it should just be enforced.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes this needs a PreToolUse hook (automatic enforcement, not user-invoked). Creates a hook script that intercepts Bash tool calls, checks if the command contains 'git push' targeting main/master, and exits non-zero to block it. Provides settings.json registration under PreToolUse with Bash matcher.
expectations: Chooses a hook (not a skill) because the user wants automatic enforcement
Chooses PreToolUse event (not PostToolUse) because it must block before execution
Provides a shell script that checks git push commands for main/master patterns
Uses exit 2 or non-zero exit to block the tool call
Includes exit 0 for non-matching commands to avoid accidental blocking
